results.resolved_title: Agrobacterium tumefaciens Deploys a Superfamily of Type VI Secretion DNase Effectors as Weapons for Interbacterial Competition In Planta
results.quote: The Atu4347-Atu4346 pair belongs to the family type VI secretion amidase effector and immunity (Tae-Tai), and the toxin likely targets the peptidoglycan.
results.note: Tae = Atu4347; text also states Tae was 'readily secreted' and is one of the three T6SS toxins (Tae, Tde1, Tde2) in A. tumefaciens C58.

results.resolved_title: Agrobacterium tumefaciens Deploys a Superfamily of Type VI Secretion DNase Effectors as Weapons for Interbacterial Competition In Planta
results.quote: the secretion of Atu4350 is indeed readily detectable... The secretion of Atu4350 is T6SS dependent, since it was abolished in a T6SS mutant
results.note: Tde1 = Atu4350, the T6SS-dependent DNase effector central to the paper, in A. tumefaciens C58.

results.resolved_title: Agrobacterium tumefaciens Deploys a Superfamily of Type VI Secretion DNase Effectors as Weapons for Interbacterial Competition In Planta
results.quote: Atu4350 and Atu3640 represent a family of T6SS toxins and are named Tde1 and Tde2, respectively
results.note: Tde2 = Atu3640, homolog of Tde1 with C-terminal toxin_43 domain; one of three A. tumefaciens C58 T6SS effectors.

results.resolved_title: Reactive oxygen species induced by Streptococcus pyogenes invasion trigger apoptotic cell death in infected epithelial cells.
results.quote: Streptococcus pyogenes (group A streptococcus, GAS), one of the most common pathogens of humans, attaches and invades into human pharyngeal or skin epithelial cells.
results.note: DOI resolves (PubMed PMID 20070306 + CrossRef) to Aikawa et al. 2010, a S. pyogenes ROS/apoptosis paper. No mention of EPEC, Cif, or T3SS. Provenance defect: cited DOI is the wrong paper.

results.resolved_title: Revisiting the functions of periplasmic chaperones in the quality control of the autotransporter Ag43 using a phenotypically homogeneous Escherichia coli strain.
results.quote: Antigen 43 is a surface-displayed autotransporter protein that mediates bacterial self-association and pathogenicity.
results.note: flu/b2000 = Antigen 43 (Ag43); autotransporter = T5SS. Paper is on E. coli Ag43 biogenesis; MeSH includes 'Type V Secretion Systems'.
